person.name: Alexander Lindsay - Lord Glenesk
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Sir Alexander Lindsay of Glenesk (died 1381) was a Scottish knight banneret. Active in jousting and as a crusader he was in favour with the Scottish kings David II and Robert II.

«b»Biography«/b»
Lindsay was the second surviving son to Sir David de Lindsay of Crawford and the Byres, and Mary Abernethy, widow of Andre de Leschelyn (Leslie), and a daughter and co-heiress of Alexander de Abernethy. Lindsay's father had been Constable of Edinburgh Castle and Berwick and active during the Wars of Independence.

Lindsay was esquire to his cousin Thomas Stewart, 2nd Earl of Angus. Lindsay inherited his mother's lands in Angus, and also acquired some of the baronies allotted to his aunt Marget Aberhethy, Countess of Angus. Upon his marriage to Catherine Stirling around 1358, he consolidated his already large maternal inheritance, with that of his wife, which would add to the extensive landholdings of the Earldom of Crawford in north-east Scotland and elsewhere.

Lindsay was knighted before 1368, He was party to a truce with England as "Chevalier et Baron" in 1369. He sealed with his nephew the settlements of the Crown (1371-1373) and was Justiciary in the North possibly as early of 1371 but definitely by 1373.

He had many safe-conducts from the English kings Edward III and Richard II, and on December 1381, he obtained a passport to travel through England on a pilgrimage to the Holy Land. He died on Crete some time before March 1382.

«b»Family«/b»
Lindsay married Catherine Stirling (died by 1378), widow of Andrew Leslie of that Ilk and daughter to John Stirling of Glenesk. They had children:

1.) David Lindsay, 1st Earl of Crawford.

2.) Sir Alexander Lindsay of Baltrody. He fought in a tournament with Ralph de Nevill 1391, a warrant to this effect being granted by King Richard II 20 June 1391. He had a pension from Crail, the entries in the Exchequer Rolls proving that he died between 1397 and 1398. He probably died childless.

3.) unnamed daughter, who married David Stewart, Earl of Strathearn

Lindsay married secondly to Marjorie, daughter to Sir John Stewart of Ralston, and niece to Robert II of Scotland.
They had children:

1.) Sir William Lindsay of Rossie, known as one of those responsible for the death of David, Duke of Rothesay. He was tutor to David, younger son of the Earl of Crawford in 1407, executor to the Earl, had charter of lands in Ballenbreich from Earl Alexander in 1423. He married Matilda Stewart (died 1485). He died between 1435 and 1437.

2.) Sir Walter Lindsay of Kinneff who was killed at the battle of Verneuil (1424). Katherine his widow married Walter Dempster. Sir Walter had a son also Walter 

3.) Euphemia Lindsay who was engaged to David Duke of Rothesay, but the marriage did not take place.

Lindsay also had an illegitimate son Jon who was living in 1378. He also had a son James, Rector of St. Brioc, Canon and Treasurer of Aberdeen. He had dispensation for illegitimacy on taking Holy Orders, but as Sir Alexander Lindsay and his second wife must have been cousins James may have been their son.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Alexander Lindsay - Lord Glenesk於1329年出生在英國蘇格蘭藍納可爾克勞福德時，他的父親David Lindsay Lord of Crawford30歲，母親Maria De Abernathy41歲。他於大約1358年與Katherine Stirling在英國蘇格蘭安格斯結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1381年10月在巴西奧 保羅朋塔爾坎迪亞享年52歲。

nameMeanings.name: Lindsay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Lindsey in Lincolnshire, England. This is first found in the form Lindissi, apparently a derivative of the British name of Lincoln . To this was later added the Old English element ēg ‘island’, since the place was virtually cut off by the surrounding fenland. The surname was taken to Scotland at an early date and is the name of an important and powerful Scottish family.
Irish: adopted as an equivalent ofvarious Gaelic names (see   Lindsey  ).

variants.name: Linzy
meanings: Altered form of Scottish, Irish, or English   Lindsay   or   Lindsey  . Compare   Lenzy  .

variants.name: Linzey
meanings: English: variant of   Lindsay   or   Lindsey  .

variants.name: Lindsey
meanings: Irish: shortened and Anglicized form of various Gaelic surnames, as for example Ó Loingsigh (see Lynch 1), Mac Giolla Fhionntóg (see McClintock ), and Ó Fhloinn (see Flynn and Lynn ).

variants.name: Lincoln
meanings: English: habitational name from the city of Lincoln, so named from an original British name Lindo- ‘lake’ + Latin colonia ‘settlement, colony’. The place was an important administrative center during the Roman occupation of Britain and in the Middle Ages it was a center for the manufacture of cloth, including the famous ‘Lincoln green’.
African American: adoption of the surname in 1 above, in honor of Abraham Lincoln, the 16th president of the US, who abolished slavery.
History: Abraham Lincoln (1809–65), 16th president of the US, was the son of an illiterate laborer, descended from Samuel Lincoln, who had emigrated from England to MA in 1637.

variants.name: Linsey
meanings: Scottish, Irish, or English: variant of   Lindsay   or   Lindsey  .
Americanized form of German Linse or Swiss German Linsi, see Lins .

nameMeanings.name: Alexander
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

en.lifeSummary: When Alexander Lindsay - Lord Glenesk was born in 1329, in Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, David Lindsay Lord of Crawford, was 30 and his mother, Maria De Abernathy, was 41. He married Katherine Stirling about 1358, in Angus, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. He died about October 1381, in Candia, Pontal, São Paulo, Brazil, at the age of 52.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alexander Lindsay - Lord Glenesk est né en 1329, à Crawford, Lanarkshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, David Lindsay Lord of Crawford, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Maria De Abernathy, avait 41 ans. Il a épousé Katherine Stirling environ 1358, à Angus, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé environ octobre 1381, à Candia, Pontal, São Paulo, Brésil, à l’âge de 52 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alexander Lindsay - Lord Glenesk 1329 in Crawford, Lanarkshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater David Lindsay Lord of Crawford 30 und seine Mutter Maria De Abernathy 41. Er hat um 1358 in Angus, Schottland, United Kingdom Katherine Stirling geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb um Oktober 1381 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Candia, Pontal, São Paulo, Brasilien.

ja.lifeSummary: Alexander Lindsay - Lord Gleneskが1329年にイギリススコットランドラナルクシュアイアクラウフォードで生まれた時，父David Lindsay Lord of Crawfordは30歳，母Maria De Abernathyは41歳であった。約1358年にイギリススコットランドアンガスでKatherine Stirlingと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は約1381年10月にブラジルスaオ プアウロポンタルカンディアで52歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Alexander Lindsay - Lord Glenesk(이)가 1329년 영국 스코틀랜드 랜아르크실르 크롤퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 David Lindsay Lord of Crawford(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Maria De Abernathy(은)는 41세였다. 그는 대략 1358년 영국 스코틀랜드 앤구스에서 Katherine Stirling(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1381년 10월 브라질 스a오 폴오 폰탤 캔디아에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alexander Lindsay - Lord Glenesk родился в 1329 г. в местности кравфорд, ланаркшир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени David Lindsay Lord of Crawford было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Maria De Abernathy, 41. Он женился на женщине по имени Katherine Stirling около 1358 г. в местности ангус, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер около октябрь 1381 г. в местности кандя, понталь, сaо поулоу, Бразилия в возрасте 52 лет.
